reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 FP gates passed. Gate 1 (dead code): ComputeForkDigest has active production call sites including bootnode.go:221. Gate 2 (trust boundary): Attack path is via untrusted P2P network (bootnode discovery), not trusted components. Gate 3 (scope): EIP-7892 blob parameter fork isolation is explicitly in-scope for Fusaka audit. An attacker can trigger this via bootnode discovery by operating a Fulu bootnode with same ForkVersion but different MaxBlobsPerBlock, causing victim nodes to compute identical fork digests and join an incompatible network, leading to consensus failures and potential chain split affecting Prysm's ~31% market share.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7892: Blob Parameter Only Hardforks requires fork digests to differ when blob parameters change. The network schedule (config.go:576-590) correctly implements this via XOR with hash(epoch||max_blobs), but ComputeForkDigest function bypasses this logic, using only fork_version and genesis_validators_root per the old spec.
